title: Compare Cisco Wireless Architectures and AP modes

questions.question: You require a density of 100 wireless clients in a relatively small area. Which design would be optimal?
questions.explanation: A high-density design is best served by lightweight APs controlled by a WLC.

correct.explanation: A high-density design is best served by lightweight APs controlled by a WLC.

incorrect.explanation: "Autonomous WAPs with a WLC" is incorrect because the scenario requires: A high-density design is best served by lightweight APs controlled by a WLC.

questions.question: Which statement is correct about Flex Connect mode versus Local mode?
choices: Flex Connect mode creates a CAPWAP tunnel to the WLC to transport data.
Local mode creates a CAPWAP tunnel to the WLC to transport data.
Local mode allows for switching of VLANs at the WAP.
Flex Connect mode allows for switching of VLANs at the WLC.
questions.explanation: Local mode sends data through CAPWAP to the WLC, while FlexConnect can switch traffic locally.

correct.explanation: Local mode sends data through CAPWAP to the WLC, while FlexConnect can switch traffic locally.

incorrect.explanation: "Flex Connect mode creates a CAPWAP tunnel to the WLC to transport data." is incorrect because the scenario requires: Local mode sends data through CAPWAP to the WLC, while FlexConnect can switch traffic locally.

incorrect.explanation: "Local mode allows for switching of VLANs at the WAP." is incorrect because the scenario requires: Local mode sends data through CAPWAP to the WLC, while FlexConnect can switch traffic locally.

incorrect.explanation: "Flex Connect mode allows for switching of VLANs at the WLC." is incorrect because the scenario requires: Local mode sends data through CAPWAP to the WLC, while FlexConnect can switch traffic locally.
